calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-26-2023 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-13-2022 Set For Trial On Jul-31-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-08-2022 Continued To Oct-26-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. RACHEL H. ROSENFELD, Esq. of Heisler Rosenfeld LLP (Tel: 925-386-6885) for and with plaintiff OCTAVIA LAZARRE;
Atty. PATRICIA L. BONHEYO, Esq. of Goodman Neuman Hamilton LLP (Tel: 415-705-0400) for defendants Lindquist, Von Husen & Joyce, LLP and Mark Brittain.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LINDQUIST, VON HUSEN & JOYCE, LLP, and MARK BRITTAIN's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled.

The demurrer asserts three grounds, each unavailing.

Statute of Limitations. Plaintiff adequately pleads that defendants failed to disclose key information to her, delaying her awareness of their malpractice to 2021. (See, e.g., FAC 2:14-3:8; Neel v. Magna, Olney, Levy, Cathcart & Gelfand (1971) 6 Cal.3d 176, 187-90.)

Unclean Hands. Defendants' unpled allegation that plaintiff "intended to violate the tax laws" raises fact issues that cannot be resolved at the pleading stage.

General Release. Defendants ask the court to interpret an extrinsic settlement agreement; this too is inappropriate at the pleading stage.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-20-2022 Continued To Sep-27-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS LINDQUIST, VON HUSEN & JOYCE, LLP and MARK BRITTAIN'S DEMURRER to 1st Amended COMPLAINT:

Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint is overruled, with leave to re-file with a proper notice of demurrer.

First, two of the four grounds for demurrer are asserted on behalf of an "Armanino," who/which apparently has no connection to this case.

Second, all four grounds simply repeat the language of CCP 430.10(e) with no elucidation. This is inadequate. The Code of Civil Procedure mandates: "A demurrer shall distinctly specify the grounds upon which any of the objections to the complaint are taken. Unless it does so, it may be disregarded." (CCP 430.60.) "Each ground of [a] demurrer must be in a separate paragraph and must state whether it applies to the entire complaint or to specified causes of action." (CRC 3.1320(a).) Where, as here, there are there are multiple defendants, "the demurrer should specify on which defendant's or defendants' behalf each ground is asserted." (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 7:107.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
